research_summary: Based on available data to mid-2025: East Auckland (Howick, Botany, Pakuranga, Flat Bush, Dannemora) median house prices ranged ~$1.05M–$1.35M depending on suburb. The market had been softening from 2022 peaks but showing stabilisation signals through early-mid 2025, with Flat Bush and Botany attracting strong first-home buyer and investor interest due to relative affordability vs. inner suburbs. Days on market were rising, giving buyers more negotiating power. OCR cuts by the RBNZ through late 2024 were beginning to lift sentiment.
hook: If you've been sitting on the sidelines in East Auckland — THIS is the window buyers have been waiting for.
script: Right now in suburbs like Flat Bush, Botany, and Pakuranga, properties are sitting on the market longer than they have in years. That means one thing for buyers: LEVERAGE. We're seeing vendors who are motivated, price drops before auction, and interest rates finally heading in the right direction after the RBNZ cuts. Median prices in parts of East Auckland are still well below the 2021 peaks — we're talking $200K–$300K lower in some pockets. If you've got your pre-approval sorted, you could be negotiating from a position of real strength right now. This won't last forever. When confidence returns fully, so do the multiple-offer situations. Don't wait to buy real estate — buy real estate, then wait.
caption:
East Auckland buyers — the tables have turned 👀

Longer days on market. Motivated vendors. Rates moving down. Flat Bush & Botany are looking like serious value right now compared to where prices were in 2021.

If you've been waiting for the 'right time' — your pre-approval could be your golden ticket this season.
